feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: OData Service field empty when creating Analytical List Page from Custom Analytical Query S/4 Public
entry.content:
System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition — Release [2602]

Context:

I built a commercial margin analysis report following the standard key user extensibility flow:

  1. Custom CDS View (Analytical Cube scenario) on top of I_SalesOrderItemCube, associated with I_SalesDocument, I_FiscalCalendarDate, I_CustomerSalesArea, and I_SalesDocItemPricingElement.

     

  2. Custom Analytical Query built on that view, with calculated measures (weighted %Margin) and several dimensions.

     

  3. The query is Published (green status) and works correctly in Preview and in the Query Browser / embedded SAC view — I validated the calculations extensively and everything matches.

The problem:

When trying to create a KPI or Analytical List Page artifact from the "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" app / View Browser, the "OData Service" field appears empty in the dropdown (both when searching by CDS View and when searching directly by OData Service), preventing me from completing the artifact creation. No service appears under "Recently Published InA Services" either (shows "No data"), even though the query itself shows as published.

 

My question:

Is there an additional explicit step required to generate the internal InA/OData service that "Manage KPIs and Reports" consumes from a Custom Analytical Query, or is this an expected limitation for this scenario (Analytical Cube + multiple associated data sources)? Has anyone successfully created a KPI or ALP from a query with this kind of configuration?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: API in public cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone 

I have a task with third party in public cloud system want to give him all materials and stock , is there an standard api I can use 

thanks

entry.title: Unable to retrieve the price list region-wise or material-wise.
entry.content: As a part of third party integration. Price list is requested by them but unable to retrieve price list region wise or material wise. Kindly help me.

entry.title: Duplicate GST Validation Triggered for Unregistered Vendors Using "NA" as GST Number(Public Cloud Sy
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Based on the recommendations provided in the SAP Community, we have implemented duplicate GST number validation by configuring the following:

  • SSCUI 102415 – Maintain Tax Number Categories

  • SSCUI 103601 – Change Message Control for Duplicate Tax Numbers

The duplicate validation is working as expected. The system prevents the creation of a new Business Partner when the GST Registration Number (Tax Category IN3) already exists for another Customer or Supplier.

However, we are facing an issue with unregistered vendors.

Current Business Process

For unregistered vendors, the business team:

  1. Creates a new Supplier Business Partner.

  2. Selects Tax Number Category = IN3 (GST Registration Number).

  3. Enters "NA" in the GST Number field, since the vendor is not GST registered.

As all unregistered vendors are maintained with the value "NA", the system considers it as a duplicate GST number and prevents the creation of subsequent unregistered vendors.

Our Question

What is the SAP standard approach for handling GST-unregistered vendors in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Specifically:

  • Is it recommended not to maintain Tax Category IN3 for GST-unregistered vendors?

  • Is there any standard configuration or business practice to allow multiple unregistered vendors without triggering duplicate validation?

  • Is there any alternative tax number category or indicator that should be used instead of maintaining "NA" under Tax Category IN3?

We would appreciate your guidance on the recommended SAP standard practice for this scenario.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Title: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Automatic Payments (F0770): how to post the payment directly W
entry.content:
Title: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Automatic Payments (F0770): how to post the payment directly WITHOUT creating a payment order

Environment
- SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Release: <fill in, e.g. 2602>)
- App: Manage Automatic Payments – Fiori ID F0770

Current behavior
When I execute a payment run in Manage Automatic Payments (F0770), the supplier open items are NOT cleared at execution time. Instead they seem to be reserved as a payment order.
As a result, when I try to clear the same items manually in Post Outgoing Payments (F1612), the action is disabled and I get:
"Item(s) 1 are part of a payment order and cannot be selected for clearing"
(This matches KBA 3503165.)
So my current payment method appears to be running in "payment order" mode.

What I want to achieve
I want the payment run to DIRECTLY post the payment document at execution time and immediately clear the supplier open item (posting the offset to the bank clearing / bank sub account) — i.e. I do NOT want a payment order to be created.

What I have checked so far (for reference only – not sure this is correct)
I suspect the "Payment order only" flag at the country-level payment method settings is involved (possibly SSCUI 101972 – Set Up Payment Methods for Each Country/Region for Payment Transactions), but I am not sure whether this flag is editable / the correct lever in Public Edition.

My question
Could someone provide the concrete, step-by-step configuration procedure to achieve DIRECT posting (no payment order) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, WITH the specific SSCUI IDs and Fiori App IDs for each step?
Specifically:
1. Exactly which setting needs to be changed, in which SSCUI (please include the SSCUI ID)?
2. What follow-on settings must be maintained (e.g. document type for payment in posting details)?
3. Any impact I should be aware of on Electronic Bank Statement clearing (since payment-order-number based matching would no longer apply)?
4. How to handle open items that are already blocked by an existing payment order?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Inbound Delivery Exceeds PO Quantity Error in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are encountering an error while creating/posting an inbound delivery against a purchase order.

Error Message:
M7 022 – Order quantity exceeded (Inbound delivery exceeds PO quantity)

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud - Automatic Printing of GR Label
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am experiencing an issue with the automatic generation of GR labels when posting a goods receipt for an inbound delivery using the Change Inbound Deliveries – Deliveries Fiori app.

Currently, the GR label is only printed when I select either the Collective Slip or Individual Slip option in the Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app. However, when the goods receipt is posted through the Change Inbound Deliveries – Deliveries, no GR label output is generated.

Could you please advise how to configure the system so that a Goods Receipt label is automatically generated and printed whenever an inbound delivery is goods receipted?

So far I've tried changing the 'Number of Slips' in MM, the parameters in the Output Parameter Determination Fiori app under Goods Movement, and changing the Assign Printout Document Type to no avail. 

Kind regards,
James

entry.title: Unable to Reverse Account Maintenance Document Due to Error FCO_AVC_PS002
entry.content:
We successfully reversed the FI document with document type KP (Account Maintenance).
After that, we attempted to reverse the corresponding Account Maintenance document using the Display/Cancel Account Maintenance Document Fiori app.
During the reversal, the system fails with the following errors:
•    FCO_AVC_PS002 – The budget tolerance limit of 100.00% for WBS element R/PMV/01/01/02/13/02 has been reached.

CB User ID - CB9980000016

entry.title: Mass Asset Change Bot and mass asset transfer between asset classes
entry.content:
Dear all, 

I would like to activate the Mass Asset Change bot for a client scheduled to go live on 01.01.2027. However, I noticed that the Mass Update Asset Master Data (570) bot, which supports mass changes, is marked for removal from the store soon. Could you please confirm when this bot will be removed and whether an alternative bot is available for mass changes to asset master data?

In addition, my client needs to transfer assets in bulk from one asset class to another. I could not find a bot in the store that supports this process, and based on the available APIs, there does not appear to be an API for this scenario either. Could you please advise how we should proceed with mass asset transfer postings?

Best regards, 

Ioanna

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud procurement and sourcing Customization ?
entry.content: Where i can learn SAP Public Cloud procurement and sourcing Customization ?

entry.title: Upload (mass create/update) capability for Fixed Assets
entry.content:
## Question 1: Upload (mass create/update) capability for Fixed Assets

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, could you please confirm whether each of the following fixed-asset operations can be performed via upload (mass data create/update) using standard functionality? Please indicate **Yes / No** for each, and if possible, the corresponding app name, migration object, and/or API.

- Master creation (creating new fixed asset master records)
- Master change (updating fields on existing master records)
- Book value adjustment
- Impairment posting
- Transfer within company code

Target version: (e.g., 2602 / latest)

entry.title: ## Question 2: App or CDS view to check the change history of Fixed Assets
entry.content:
## Question 2: App or CDS view to check the change history of Fixed Assets

Could you please confirm whether there is a standard app or CDS view to check the **change history** of fixed asset master/detail records after a change is made? Specifically, I would like to trace "who changed which value from what to what, and when" for the following fields:

- Cost center
- Book value
- Municipality code (Japan localization–specific field)

For each field, please advise the app name / CDS view name where the change history can be reviewed (or whether the field is covered by change logs / change documents).

Target version: (e.g., 2602 / latest)

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud] How to Transfer a Customer Line Item with Negative Balance to a Vendor Line Item
entry.content:
Hello,

I would appreciate your advice on the following matter.

【System Environment】
SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

【What I'm trying to achieve】
I would like to create payment data after recognizing a customer line item
with a negative balance as a payable (vendor-side liability).
As a means to achieve this, within the same Business Partner (BP), I am
planning to transfer a customer line item with a negative balance to a
vendor line item.

Rather than clearing the customer line item against the vendor line item,
I would like the following:
- Customer line item: cleared
- Vendor line item: a new open item (payable) is created

【Desired process flow】
1. A receivable (accounts receivable) is created for the customer
2. A return occurs, causing the customer balance to become negative
(credit balance)
3. The customer line item with the negative balance is transferred to a
vendor line item (payable) under the same BP code
4. Payment data is created based on the vendor line item (assuming the
Automatic Payment Program / F110)
5. A payment advice is issued

【Question】
Could you advise which app/transaction can achieve step 3 above
("transfer from customer line item to vendor line item")?
① I am considering the "Clear Incoming Payments" app (App ID: F0773) —
is this achievable with that app?
② Are there any other apps or configurations required besides the above?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Designing Product Hierarchy in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm designing Product Hierarchies in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have a question about the recommended design.

Base on my understanding:

  • A Product Hierarchy ID can be assigned to multiple Sales Organization / Distribution Channel.
  • Each Sales Organization / Distribution Channel can only have one assigned Product Hierarchy. please refer to the screenshot below

 

  • Once materials are assigned to the hierarchy, its structure (such as adding or deleting nodes) can no longer be changed.

 

Because of these limitations, I'm wondering how others handle future changes to the hierarchy structure.

Do you create a new Product Hierarchy ID when structural changes are needed? If so, is it common to treat the top-level Product Hierarchy as a version (for example, PH_V1, PH_V2, or PH_2025, PH_2026) rather than as the actual business classification?

 

Or is there another recommended design or best practice?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Request Vendor & Customer Payment Receipt Advice Print Automatic Email
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We would like to request guidance regarding the standard functionality for sending Vendor Payment Advice and Customer Payment Receipt Advice automatically via email in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Business Requirement:
Our business requires that:

  • Vendor Payment Advice is automatically generated and emailed to vendors after payment execution.
  • Customer Payment Receipt Advice is automatically generated and emailed to customers after receipt posting.
  • The advice should be available in a printable format (PDF) and sent automatically to the email address maintained in the Business Partner master data.

Request:

  1. Please confirm whether this functionality is available as standard in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
  2. If available, please provide the required configuration steps (Output Management, Email Templates, Communication Arrangements, SSCUIs, etc.).
  3. If this functionality is

entry.title: Error RW602 during Periodic Valuation posting - how to analyze?
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts!
During the periodic valuation run, I get the following error.

RW602 (Required field GL_ACCOUNT was not transferred in parameter ACCOUNTGL)

At first glance, I would think that I forgot to assign an account to an account symbol somewhere. But I can't really imagine that being the case.
It would be helpful if the error message could also tell me for which process (condition type, flow type, account determination value, account symbol, etc.) an account is missing. But the message does not have any further variables. 

Do you have any idea how to figure this out on a case-by-case basis?

Thanks in advance for any advice, and best regards,
Rado

entry.title: To check feasibility of adding column in 'COOIS' report for 'Components' in FIORI
entry.content:
I want to add an extra colum in "COOIS" "Components"
Name of the Extra column will be "Missing Qty".
"Missing Qty" should be the difference between existing columns "Requirement Qty" & "Withdrawn Qty"
Is this feasible to do this in FIORI ?

entry.title: To check feasibility of adding column in COOIS report for & Components in FIORI
entry.content:
I want to add an extra colum in "COOIS" "Components"
Name of the Extra column will be "Missing Qty".
"Missing Qty" should be the difference between existing columns "Requirement Qty" & "Withdrawn Qty"
Is this feasible to do this in FIORI ?

entry.title: Customer reference list in VA01 is shown but entries cannot be selected
entry.content:
The issue I am experiencing is that in app VA01, the list of historical entries in the customer reference field is displayed, but I cannot select any of the entries. As a result, I have to manually re-enter the values for each new customer order. I need support to resolve this problem.

(This entry was created using Joule in SAP4Me :-))

entry.title: Error related to period determination occurred while creating an asset card for a US company
entry.content:
US company code: 3010
Custom asset class: Z001
Depreciation key used under US GAAP: SUL3

The system has enabled the configurable fiscal year variant SZ and assigned it to company code 3010. However, when creating an asset card for company code 3010 with custom asset class Z001, an error occurs. What could be the cause?

For detailed configuration information, please refer to the screenshots in the attachment.

The error message is as follows:

“Period determination is not possible on date 20260708 for period control SA
Diagnosis
For depreciation key (with period control SA and period control method of phase), no depreciation period could be determined for date 20260708.”
